---
title: "Reaction-diffusion"
subtitle: "Two chemicals, a GPU, and Turing's 1952 hunch"
toc: false
sidebar: false
---

Two chemicals diffuse across a surface and react where they meet. That's the whole setup. Get the rates right and it stops being boring: the even mix goes unstable and sorts itself into spots, stripes, mazes, and blobs that split like dividing cells. Alan Turing wrote the maths down in [1952](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_chemical_basis_of_morphogenesis), as an answer to how a featureless ball of cells decides where to put a leopard's spots. It's the smooth cousin of the [Game of Life](/life/) on the other page here: same idea, local rules run in parallel until structure emerges, but continuous instead of square.

The version below is Gray-Scott. Two species, a feed rate that tops up the first, a kill rate that drains the second, and a reaction that turns one into the other. Move those two numbers and the same equations hand you coral, fingerprints, or self-replicating dots. Pick a preset and watch, or click on the canvas to drop in some chemical and start a pattern yourself.

It all runs on the GPU. The state, two concentrations per cell, lives in a floating-point texture. A single fragment shader reads each cell and its four neighbours, applies a 5-point Laplacian for the diffusion term, does the Gray-Scott reaction, and writes the next state. A shader can't read and write the same texture at once, so it ping-pongs between two of them: read A, write B, swap, repeat, a few dozen times per frame before anything reaches the screen.

```{=html}
<div class="rd-app">
  <div class="rd-controls">
    <button id="rd-play" type="button">Pause</button>
    <button id="rd-reset" type="button">Reset</button>
    <select id="rd-preset" aria-label="Pattern preset">
      <option value="coral">Coral</option>
      <option value="mitosis">Mitosis</option>
      <option value="worms">Worms</option>
      <option value="maze">Maze</option>
      <option value="spots">Spots</option>
      <option value="custom">Custom</option>
    </select>
    <label class="rd-speed">
      Speed
      <input id="rd-speed-in" type="range" min="1" max="40" step="1" value="14">
      <span id="rd-speed-out">14</span>&times;
    </label>
    <label class="rd-param">
      Feed (F)
      <input id="rd-feed" type="range" min="0" max="0.1" step="0.0005" value="0.0545">
      <output id="rd-feed-out">0.0545</output>
    </label>
    <label class="rd-param">
      Kill (k)
      <input id="rd-kill" type="range" min="0.03" max="0.075" step="0.0005" value="0.0620">
      <output id="rd-kill-out">0.0620</output>
    </label>
  </div>
  <canvas id="rd-canvas" aria-label="Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation"></canvas>
  <p class="rd-hint">Click or drag on the canvas to add chemical and seed new growth.</p>
  <p id="rd-fallback" class="rd-fallback" hidden>This needs WebGL2 with float textures, which your browser didn't provide, so the simulation can't run here.</p>
</div>

<style>
  .rd-app {
    max-width: 560px;
    margin: 1.5rem auto 2rem;
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.75rem;
  }
  .rd-controls {
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 0.5rem;
    font-size: 0.9rem;
  }
  .rd-controls button,
  .rd-controls select {
    padding: 0.3rem 0.7rem;
    border: 1px solid #d0d7de;
    background: #fff;
    border-radius: 4px;
    cursor: pointer;
    font-size: 0.9rem;
  }
  .rd-controls button:hover,
  .rd-controls select:hover { border-color: #1f6f8b; }
  .rd-controls button:active { background: #f6f8fa; }
  .rd-speed,
  .rd-param {
    display: inline-flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 0.4rem;
    margin-left: 0.25rem;
  }
  .rd-speed input,
  .rd-param input { accent-color: #1f6f8b; }
  .rd-param output {
    font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
    min-width: 3.6em;
  }
  #rd-canvas {
    width: 100%;
    height: auto;
    aspect-ratio: 1 / 1;
    background: #05070d;
    border: 1px solid #d0d7de;
    border-radius: 4px;
    cursor: crosshair;
    touch-action: none;
  }
  .rd-hint { font-size: 0.85rem; color: #666; margin: 0; }
  .rd-fallback { font-size: 0.9rem; color: #b03a3a; margin: 0; }
  @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
    .rd-controls button,
    .rd-controls select { background: #161b22; border-color: #30363d; color: #c9d1d9; }
    .rd-controls button:active { background: #21262d; }
    #rd-canvas { border-color: #30363d; }
    .rd-hint { color: #8b949e; }
  }
</style>

<script>
(() => {
  const SIM = 256;
  const canvas = document.getElementById('rd-canvas');
  const fallback = document.getElementById('rd-fallback');
  const gl = canvas && canvas.getContext('webgl2', { antialias: false });

  if (!gl || !gl.getExtension('EXT_color_buffer_float')) {
    if (canvas) canvas.hidden = true;
    document.querySelector('.rd-controls')?.setAttribute('hidden', '');
    document.querySelector('.rd-hint')?.setAttribute('hidden', '');
    if (fallback) fallback.hidden = false;
    return;
  }

  canvas.width = SIM;
  canvas.height = SIM;

  const VS = `#version 300 es
  in vec2 a_pos;
  out vec2 v_uv;
  void main() {
    v_uv = a_pos * 0.5 + 0.5;
    gl_Position = vec4(a_pos, 0.0, 1.0);
  }`;

  const SIM_FS = `#version 300 es
  precision highp float;
  precision highp sampler2D;
  uniform sampler2D u_state;
  uniform vec2 u_texel;
  uniform float u_dA, u_dB, u_feed, u_kill, u_dt;
  in vec2 v_uv;
  out vec4 outColor;
  void main() {
    vec2 c = texture(u_state, v_uv).rg;
    vec2 lap = texture(u_state, v_uv + vec2(u_texel.x, 0.0)).rg
             + texture(u_state, v_uv - vec2(u_texel.x, 0.0)).rg
             + texture(u_state, v_uv + vec2(0.0, u_texel.y)).rg
             + texture(u_state, v_uv - vec2(0.0, u_texel.y)).rg
             - 4.0 * c;
    float A = c.r, B = c.g;
    float reaction = A * B * B;
    float nA = A + (u_dA * lap.r - reaction + u_feed * (1.0 - A)) * u_dt;
    float nB = B + (u_dB * lap.g + reaction - (u_kill + u_feed) * B) * u_dt;
    outColor = vec4(clamp(nA, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(nB, 0.0, 1.0), 0.0, 1.0);
  }`;

  const SPLAT_FS = `#version 300 es
  precision highp float;
  precision highp sampler2D;
  uniform sampler2D u_state;
  uniform vec2 u_point;
  uniform float u_radius;
  in vec2 v_uv;
  out vec4 outColor;
  void main() {
    vec2 c = texture(u_state, v_uv).rg;
    if (distance(v_uv, u_point) < u_radius) { c.r = 0.0; c.g = 0.9; }
    outColor = vec4(c, 0.0, 1.0);
  }`;

  const DISP_FS = `#version 300 es
  precision highp float;
  precision highp sampler2D;
  uniform sampler2D u_state;
  in vec2 v_uv;
  out vec4 outColor;
  void main() {
    float B = texture(u_state, v_uv).g;
    vec3 col = vec3(0.04, 0.05, 0.10);
    col = mix(col, vec3(0.10, 0.42, 0.52), smoothstep(0.08, 0.22, B));
    col = mix(col, vec3(0.97, 0.96, 0.90), smoothstep(0.22, 0.40, B));
    outColor = vec4(col, 1.0);
  }`;

  function compile(type, src) {
    const s = gl.createShader(type);
    gl.shaderSource(s, src);
    gl.compileShader(s);
    if (!gl.getShaderParameter(s, gl.COMPILE_STATUS)) console.error(gl.getShaderInfoLog(s));
    return s;
  }
  function program(fs) {
    const p = gl.createProgram();
    gl.attachShader(p, compile(gl.VERTEX_SHADER, VS));
    gl.attachShader(p, compile(gl.FRAGMENT_SHADER, fs));
    gl.bindAttribLocation(p, 0, 'a_pos');
    gl.linkProgram(p);
    if (!gl.getProgramParameter(p, gl.LINK_STATUS)) console.error(gl.getProgramInfoLog(p));
    return p;
  }

  const simProg = program(SIM_FS);
  const splatProg = program(SPLAT_FS);
  const dispProg = program(DISP_FS);

  const vao = gl.createVertexArray();
  gl.bindVertexArray(vao);
  gl.bindBuffer(gl.ARRAY_BUFFER, gl.createBuffer());
  gl.bufferData(gl.ARRAY_BUFFER, new Float32Array([-1, -1, 3, -1, -1, 3]), gl.STATIC_DRAW);
  gl.enableVertexAttribArray(0);
  gl.vertexAttribPointer(0, 2, gl.FLOAT, false, 0, 0);

  function makeTex() {
    const t = gl.createTexture();
    gl.bindTexture(gl.TEXTURE_2D, t);
    gl.texParameteri(gl.TEXTURE_2D, gl.TEXTURE_MIN_FILTER, gl.NEAREST);
    gl.texParameteri(gl.TEXTURE_2D, gl.TEXTURE_MAG_FILTER, gl.NEAREST);
    gl.texParameteri(gl.TEXTURE_2D, gl.TEXTURE_WRAP_S, gl.REPEAT);
    gl.texParameteri(gl.TEXTURE_2D, gl.TEXTURE_WRAP_T, gl.REPEAT);
    gl.texImage2D(gl.TEXTURE_2D, 0, gl.RGBA32F, SIM, SIM, 0, gl.RGBA, gl.FLOAT, null);
    return t;
  }
  let texA = makeTex();
  let texB = makeTex();
  const fbo = gl.createFramebuffer();

  const sl = {
    state: gl.getUniformLocation(simProg, 'u_state'),
    texel: gl.getUniformLocation(simProg, 'u_texel'),
    dA: gl.getUniformLocation(simProg, 'u_dA'),
    dB: gl.getUniformLocation(simProg, 'u_dB'),
    feed: gl.getUniformLocation(simProg, 'u_feed'),
    kill: gl.getUniformLocation(simProg, 'u_kill'),
    dt: gl.getUniformLocation(simProg, 'u_dt'),
  };
  const pl = {
    state: gl.getUniformLocation(splatProg, 'u_state'),
    point: gl.getUniformLocation(splatProg, 'u_point'),
    radius: gl.getUniformLocation(splatProg, 'u_radius'),
  };
  const dl = { state: gl.getUniformLocation(dispProg, 'u_state') };

  const PRESETS = {
    coral:   { feed: 0.0545, kill: 0.0620 },
    mitosis: { feed: 0.0367, kill: 0.0649 },
    worms:   { feed: 0.0580, kill: 0.0630 },
    maze:    { feed: 0.0290, kill: 0.0570 },
    spots:   { feed: 0.0250, kill: 0.0600 },
  };
  const params = { dA: 0.16, dB: 0.08, dt: 1.0, feed: PRESETS.coral.feed, kill: PRESETS.coral.kill };

  function seed() {
    const data = new Float32Array(SIM * SIM * 4);
    for (let i = 0; i < SIM * SIM; i++) { data[i * 4] = 1.0; data[i * 4 + 3] = 1.0; }
    const stamp = (cx, cy, r) => {
      for (let y = -r; y <= r; y++) for (let x = -r; x <= r; x++) {
        const px = (cx + x + SIM) % SIM, py = (cy + y + SIM) % SIM;
        const idx = (py * SIM + px) * 4;
        data[idx] = 0.5; data[idx + 1] = 0.25;
      }
    };
    stamp(SIM >> 1, SIM >> 1, 10);
    for (let k = 0; k < 6; k++) stamp((Math.random() * SIM) | 0, (Math.random() * SIM) | 0, 6);
    gl.bindTexture(gl.TEXTURE_2D, texA);
    gl.texImage2D(gl.TEXTURE_2D, 0, gl.RGBA32F, SIM, SIM, 0, gl.RGBA, gl.FLOAT, data);
  }

  function pass(prog, setup) {
    gl.bindFramebuffer(gl.FRAMEBUFFER, fbo);
    gl.framebufferTexture2D(gl.FRAMEBUFFER, gl.COLOR_ATTACHMENT0, gl.TEXTURE_2D, texB, 0);
    gl.viewport(0, 0, SIM, SIM);
    gl.useProgram(prog);
    gl.activeTexture(gl.TEXTURE0);
    gl.bindTexture(gl.TEXTURE_2D, texA);
    setup();
    gl.bindVertexArray(vao);
    gl.drawArrays(gl.TRIANGLES, 0, 3);
    const tmp = texA; texA = texB; texB = tmp;
  }

  function step() {
    pass(simProg, () => {
      gl.uniform1i(sl.state, 0);
      gl.uniform2f(sl.texel, 1 / SIM, 1 / SIM);
      gl.uniform1f(sl.dA, params.dA);
      gl.uniform1f(sl.dB, params.dB);
      gl.uniform1f(sl.feed, params.feed);
      gl.uniform1f(sl.kill, params.kill);
      gl.uniform1f(sl.dt, params.dt);
    });
  }

  function splat(x, y) {
    pass(splatProg, () => {
      gl.uniform1i(pl.state, 0);
      gl.uniform2f(pl.point, x, y);
      gl.uniform1f(pl.radius, 0.03);
    });
  }

  function render() {
    gl.bindFramebuffer(gl.FRAMEBUFFER, null);
    gl.viewport(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
    gl.useProgram(dispProg);
    gl.activeTexture(gl.TEXTURE0);
    gl.bindTexture(gl.TEXTURE_2D, texA);
    gl.uniform1i(dl.state, 0);
    gl.bindVertexArray(vao);
    gl.drawArrays(gl.TRIANGLES, 0, 3);
  }

  let running = true;
  let stepsPerFrame = 14;
  let painting = false;

  function loop() {
    if (running) for (let i = 0; i < stepsPerFrame; i++) step();
    render();
    requestAnimationFrame(loop);
  }

  const playBtn = document.getElementById('rd-play');
  playBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
    running = !running;
    playBtn.textContent = running ? 'Pause' : 'Play';
  });
  document.getElementById('rd-reset').addEventListener('click', seed);
  const presetSel = document.getElementById('rd-preset');
  const feedIn = document.getElementById('rd-feed');
  const killIn = document.getElementById('rd-kill');
  const feedOut = document.getElementById('rd-feed-out');
  const killOut = document.getElementById('rd-kill-out');
  const fmt = (v) => v.toFixed(4);
  function syncParamUI() {
    feedIn.value = params.feed;
    killIn.value = params.kill;
    feedOut.textContent = fmt(params.feed);
    killOut.textContent = fmt(params.kill);
  }
  presetSel.addEventListener('change', (e) => {
    const p = PRESETS[e.target.value];
    if (p) { params.feed = p.feed; params.kill = p.kill; syncParamUI(); }
  });
  feedIn.addEventListener('input', () => {
    params.feed = +feedIn.value;
    feedOut.textContent = fmt(params.feed);
    presetSel.value = 'custom';
  });
  killIn.addEventListener('input', () => {
    params.kill = +killIn.value;
    killOut.textContent = fmt(params.kill);
    presetSel.value = 'custom';
  });
  syncParamUI();
  const speedIn = document.getElementById('rd-speed-in');
  const speedOut = document.getElementById('rd-speed-out');
  speedIn.addEventListener('input', () => { stepsPerFrame = +speedIn.value; speedOut.textContent = speedIn.value; });

  function uvOf(e) {
    const r = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
    return [(e.clientX - r.left) / r.width, 1.0 - (e.clientY - r.top) / r.height];
  }
  canvas.addEventListener('pointerdown', (e) => {
    painting = true;
    const [x, y] = uvOf(e); splat(x, y);
    canvas.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId);
  });
  canvas.addEventListener('pointermove', (e) => {
    if (!painting) return;
    const [x, y] = uvOf(e); splat(x, y);
  });
  canvas.addEventListener('pointerup', () => { painting = false; });

  seed();
  requestAnimationFrame(loop);
})();
</script>
```

## The two knobs

Everything on screen is steered by two numbers: the feed rate *F* and the kill rate *k*. Most of the (*F*, *k*) plane is dead, the pattern either floods solid or starves to nothing. The interesting behaviour lives on a thin band between those fates, and small steps along it flip you between mazes, drifting spots, and mitosis. The presets drop you onto known-good points, and the *F* and *k* sliders let you wander off them and find your own; the links below map the rest.

## Further reading

- [Karl Sims, *Reaction-Diffusion Tutorial*](https://www.karlsims.com/rd.html) — the clearest walk-through of the equations and what each term does.
- [Robert Munafo's xmorphia](https://mrob.com/pub/comp/xmorphia/) — the exhaustive (*F*, *k*) atlas: which numbers give which creatures.
- [pmneila's WebGL Gray-Scott](https://pmneila.github.io/jsexp/grayscott/) — the in-browser demo that made it obvious you could run this on the GPU.

[← back to haigh.bio](/)

*Turing would've loved a fast GPU.*
